frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment:
A: Har baat mein 'men are unsafe' bol dena bhi theek nahi hai yaar. Sab mard ek jaise nahi hote.
B: Exactly. Main khud beti ka baap hoon, aur kabhi kisi aurat ke saath galat behave nahi kiya. Phir bhi agar koi woman seedha mujhe suspicious samjhe, toh thoda insulting lagta hai.
A: Precaution alag baat hai, but har aadmi ko potential threat treat karna bhi fair nahi hai. Men ki side bhi kabhi samjho.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering male hurt and demanding trust from women. It encodes the classic 'not all men' response, personal exception-making ('I have never done anything'), and framing women's wariness as unfair to men.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Bhopal. You are sitting with four male colleagues in the office canteen during lunch. One colleague is telling the group that his 25 year old daughter told him she is in a live-in relationship with her boyfriend. You are one of four men reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on live-in relationships and what a father's responsibility is when his daughter makes choices like this and what it says about how she was raised.
